claim: The Currier A/B split encodes two different plaintext languages (e.g., Latin in sections assigned to A, Italian or an Italian dialect in sections assigned to B), detectable by different glyph-level entropy and different word-initial/final bigram profiles between A and B corpora.
evidence_for: Currier B corpus (23,766 words) is more than twice the size of A (11,022 words), consistent with a dominant vs. supplementary language split
Prior hypothesis H004/H008 both carry elevated confidence (0.808, 0.697) for genuine linguistic difference
Latin glyph entropy ~4.0 bits and Italian ~3.95 bits bracket the observed 3.8627 bits, suggesting a blend or dialect
evidence_against: Overall glyph entropy 3.8627 sits below both Latin and Italian benchmarks, making a clean two-language split harder to reconcile without invoking encoding overhead
If two languages were used, section-level entropy should show bimodal distribution, but the range (3.71-3.90) is unimodal and narrow
High shared vocabulary between A and B would be unlikely if languages differ substantially
test: Partition the corpus into Currier-A and Currier-B tokens. Compute per-character (glyph) entropy, word-initial bigram frequency table, and word-final bigram frequency table separately for each partition. Run chi-squared test on the bigram distributions.
test_metric: Glyph entropy difference between A and B; chi-squared statistic on word-initial and word-final bigram tables; Jensen-Shannon divergence of unigram glyph distributions.
test_threshold: Confirmation: entropy difference > 0.05 bits AND chi-squared p < 0.01 on bigram tables AND JS divergence > 0.05 nats. Denial: all three metrics fall below thresholds simultaneously.

tests_run.test: partition the corpus into currier-a and currier-b tokens. compute per-character (glyph) entropy, wor
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: Confirming a genuine language split would allow separate decipherment tracks for A and B, halving effective search space; denial would redirect effort toward scribal or stylistic explanations.
